\u03b2_005.statement: The construction of models of PA where P = NP holds suggests the possibility of formal independence but does not establish it for ZFC

\u03b1_039_revised.statement: Lower bounds in algebraic proof systems might inform model construction strategies through concrete hard instances

\u03b1_001.statement: Ben-David & Halevi (1992) constructed a model of PA where P = NP holds

\u03b1_003.statement: The Krajíček-Pudlák correspondence establishes that superpolynomial proof complexity lower bounds for all proof systems imply NP ⊄ coNP/poly

\u03b1_004.statement: Proving P ≠ NP in PA would require proving superpolynomial lower bounds for all proof systems
evidence: Consequence of α_003 and formalization principles

\u03b1_005.statement: PA cannot prove the totality of certain superexponential functions

\u03b1_006.statement: Natural proofs cannot separate P from NP under standard cryptographic assumptions

\u03b1_007.statement: There exist oracles A, B such that P^A = NP^A and P^B ≠ NP^B

\u03b1_008.statement: The convergence of relativization, natural proofs, and algebraization barriers constitutes evidence for formal independence of P ≠ NP

\u03b1_009.statement: A complete independence proof requires constructing a model of PA where P ≠ NP holds

\u03b1_010.statement: The P vs NP question is formally independent of PA
evidence: missing only the P ≠ NP model

\u03b2_001.statement: The P vs NP problem is subject to the relativization barrier, which prevents any relativizing proof technique from resolving it

\u03b2_002.statement: The natural proofs barrier suggests that no natural proof technique can establish NP ≠ P under standard cryptographic assumptions

\u03b2_003.statement: The algebraization barrier extends the relativization barrier to algebraic settings, indicating that algebraic methods alone cannot resolve the P vs NP problem

\u03b2_004.statement: There is no formal proof currently that P ≠ NP is independent in standard foundational systems like ZFC

\u03b1_004_revised.statement: Any PA proof of P ≠ NP would have implications for proof complexity theory, though the exact requirements remain unclear
evidence: Consequence of α_003 and proof formalization principles

\u03b1_008_revised.statement: The convergence of barriers suggests that resolving P vs NP requires fundamentally new techniques, providing circumstantial evidence for possible independence

\u03b1_010_revised.statement: The P vs NP question shows characteristics consistent with formal independence from PA, though definitive proof remains open
evidence: α_008_revised; acknowledges missing P ≠ NP model

\u03b1_011.statement: The logical complexity of P ≠ NP as a Π₂ statement poses specific challenges for model construction that don't arise for Σ₂ statements like P = NP
evidence: Basic model theory and complexity of formula types

\u03b1_012.statement: Full independence of P ≠ NP requires constructing a model where P ≠ NP holds
evidence: Definition of independence in model theory

\u03b1_013.statement: The missing P ≠ NP model is the primary gap in establishing full independence
evidence: Synthesis: we have α_001 but lack dual construction

\u03b1_014.statement: Resolution likely requires new forcing-style techniques for complexity classes

\u03b1_015.statement: P ≠ NP shows strong evidence of independence from PA, though full independence from stronger systems remains open

\u03b1_016.statement: No known impossibility result prevents constructing a model where P ≠ NP holds
evidence: Survey of literature shows technical obstacles but no proven impossibility

\u03b1_017.statement: The convergence of barriers suggests the problem may require fundamentally new techniques
evidence: Pattern of failed approaches using standard methods

\u03b1_018.statement: The proof complexity connection provides a potential path to independence
evidence: If proving lower bounds is itself unprovable

\u03b1_019.statement: No known impossibility result prevents constructing a P ≠ NP model
evidence: Literature survey reveals obstacles but no proven impossibility

\u03b1_020.statement: Resolution of independence question requires techniques beyond current mathematical methods

\u03b1_021.statement: Resolution of the independence question remains genuinely open
evidence: Neither full independence nor its negation is established

\u03b1_006_revised.statement: Constructing models satisfying Π₂ statements requires different techniques than for Σ₂ statements

\u03b1_011_revised.statement: The convergence of independent barriers suggests structural obstacles to standard proof techniques
evidence: Three mathematically independent frameworks all fail

\u03b1_014_revised.statement: The asymmetry between Σ₂ and Π₂ model construction partially explains the missing P ≠ NP model

\u03b1_017_revised.statement: The proof complexity connection establishes sufficient conditions for consistency of P ≠ NP

\u03b1_019_revised.statement: Current evidence suggests potential independence of P vs NP, though not conclusively

\u03b1_021_revised.statement: The missing P ≠ NP model represents a significant gap in the independence argument

\u03b1_022.statement: PA-unprovability does not immediately imply ZFC-independence
evidence: Standard results in logic: many PA-unprovable statements are ZFC-provable

\u03b1_023.statement: No progress on P vs NP has been achieved by moving to theories stronger than PA
evidence: Literature survey: all results remain at PA level

\u03b1_024.statement: The barriers exclude most successful complexity-theoretic proof techniques
evidence: Analysis of barriers and existing proofs in complexity theory

\u03b1_025.statement: The barriers represent evidence of extreme difficulty, not proven independence

\u03b1_026.statement: Buss's bounded arithmetic theory S₂¹ corresponds precisely to polynomial-time computation

\u03b1_027.statement: The theory S₂² captures the computational strength of NP

\u03b1_028.statement: PA cannot prove the totality of functions definable in S₂²

\u03b1_029.statement: The inability of PA to reason about NP computations explains why PA ⊬ "P ≠ NP"

\u03b1_030.statement: Proof systems form a hierarchy corresponding to fragments of arithmetic

\u03b1_031.statement: Proving P ≠ NP requires superpolynomial lower bounds for logical-level proof systems

\u03b1_032.statement: Logical-level proof systems correspond to theories approaching PA's strength

\u03b1_033.statement: The absence of feasible interpolation in EF implies the existence of one-way functions

\u03b1_034.statement: One-way functions imply both P ≠ NP and the Natural Proofs barrier

\u03b1_035.statement: There exists a fundamental circularity between proving P ≠ NP and overcoming Natural Proofs

\u03b1_036.statement: No standard model-construction technique is suited for ensuring universal computational failure

\u03b1_037.statement: The theory V₁⁰ is conservative over IΣ₀^{1,b} for Σ₀^{1,b}-consequences

\u03b1_038.statement: Independence results in bounded arithmetic can be lifted via conservativity

\u03b1_039.statement: Lower bounds in algebraic proof systems may provide routes to model construction

\u03b1_040.statement: The minimal theory for formalizing P vs NP lies between S₂¹ and S₂²
evidence: complexity of P vs NP statement

\u03b1_041.statement: P vs NP exhibits the partial independence pattern of statements like CH

\u03b1_042.statement: The cryptographic connection creates self-reinforcing obstacles to independence proofs

\u03b1_043.statement: Bounded arithmetic provides the most promising route to completing the independence proof

\u03b1_044.statement: The proof complexity hierarchy reveals why P vs NP resists resolution in weak theories

\u03b1_045.statement: Current evidence strongly suggests but does not prove ZFC-independence of P vs NP

\u03b1_029_revised.statement: PA's inability to reason fully about NP computations provides a necessary but not sufficient explanation for why PA ⊬ "P ≠ NP"

\u03b1_035_revised.statement: Cryptographic assumptions create conditional obstacles to proving P ≠ NP via Natural Proofs

\u03b1_036_revised.statement: Current standard model-construction techniques have not been successfully adapted to ensure universal computational failure

\u03b1_042_revised.statement: Cryptographic connections create conditional obstacles to specific independence proof approaches

\u03b1_044_revised.statement: The proof complexity hierarchy identifies specific technical obstacles to resolving P vs NP in weak theories

\u03b1_045_revised.statement: Current evidence suggests but does not establish the formal independence of P vs NP from ZFC
